events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We are asked: "orange cable handling violations". This is high-voltage safety. Likely a violation of handling procedures for orange cables (which denote high voltage). The work is rough-complete. So there's a violation observed. Decision should be "fail". Risk: high or critical given HV. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: non-compliant, maybe installed-defective? Cable handling violations? Might involve improper support, routing, etc. Percent complete? It's rough-complete, maybe 80%? Value: no numeric reading. Sound source: none. Confidence: high. Event time: not applicable. Rate: not applicable. Order: none. Workable: maybe steps to correct? Findings: list the specific violations. Actions: corrective actions. Rationale: brief. References: OEM HV procedures, NFPA 70E-adjacent.

Decision: fail. Risk: high or critical? Orange cable handling violations can lead to shock or arc flash, so critical. Work stage: rough-complete. Component conditions: ["non-compliant"]. Percent complete: maybe 70-80%? I'll say 75. Value: 0. Sound source: "". Confidence: 0.95. Event time: 0. Rate: 0. Order: []. Workable: []. Findings: ["Orange HV cable handling violations observed"]. Actions: ["Cease work on HV system", "Review OEM HV cable handling procedures", "Correct installation to meet requirements", "Re-inspect before energization"]. Rationale: "Orange cables indicate high voltage; improper handling poses immediate shock and arc-flash hazards, violating OEM HV procedures and NFPA 70E-adjacent standards." References: ["OEM HV procedures", "NFPA 70E-adjacent"].
